slugRenames.toTitle: The Book of Why by Judea Pearl
slugRenames.comment: The 2022 'Book of Why' contest review opens with a Prologue titled 'Three Jews on the Internet'. The pipeline's composite-doc splitter misreads the prologue heading as a separate essay's title; the actual review is about Pearl's Book of Why. The other sibling section headings (Fisher is Evil, Ladder of Causation, Monty Hall Paradox, Epilogue) were already split into separate slugs by earlier pipeline runs and are out of scope here.

fashion-faith-and-fantasy-in-the-new-phy.comment: Author styles each section as a stacked pair of bold-only paragraphs: a short section title (Fashion/Faith/Fantasy/Conclusion or 'The Mathematical Appendix') followed by a longer, cute subtitle ('Beating Up Particle Physics and Taking its Lunch Money', etc.). The generic bold-to-H2 promoter sees the first of each pair as flanked by a bold-only line (not substantive prose) and promotes the second of each pair instead — backwards. List explicitly which lines should be H2; the rest revert to bold.

h2Lines: 4.5 Epistemology, What Is It Good For? War
4.12 The Bigger Picture Informs the Smallest
what-is-real.comment: Author uses numbered-section conventions (1., 2., 4.1, 4.10, etc.) for the actual section structure but also bolds occasional rhetorical asides — `**Did I lose you? Where? Was it the 'slow clap'? Too dramatic?**` — which the generic bold-to-H2 promoter mistakes for section headings. Only the numbered lines are real headings; the asides revert to bold.

h2Lines: 2. It still seems wrong to be willing to kill your child based on faith
fear-and-trembling.comment: Section headings are styled as plain paragraphs (no bold or Heading-N) like `1. Many father Abrahams`. The generic bold-to-H2 promoter only handles `**...**` lines; the extended applyH2Overrides matches the unescaped plain-text form here.

works-by-isaac-bickerstaff.comment: Both Part 1 and Part 2 are bold-only paragraphs with the same wrap structure in the source. The generic promoter caught Part 2 (different neighboring context) but not Part 1 (preceded by an empty paragraph that fails the substantive-prose-above check). List both explicitly so they're H2 consistently.
